edges.description: Reduced type V collagen impairs fibril nucleation and ECM tensile strength.
Collagen-poor matrices fail under repetitive motion and minor trauma.
Ligamentous laxity from weak collagen fibrils permits excessive joint excursion.
Dermal matrix weakness allows abnormal skin stretchability.
Fragile perivascular connective tissue increases capillary vulnerability.
Valve leaflet connective tissue laxity predisposes to prolapse.
Scleral connective tissue weakness can increase axial elongation.
Repetitive instability episodes result in frequent subluxations and dislocations.
Ongoing mechanical injury and microinstability lead to chronic pain.
Defective collagen remodeling slows re-epithelialization and scar strengthening.
Poor collagen deposition produces thin widened scars.
Recurrent focal trauma and disordered repair produce fibrotic nodular lesions.
Connective tissue laxity at the gastroesophageal junction can impair sphincter competence.
Joint hypermobility predisposes to repeated episodes of joint displacement.
Persistent joint pain resulting from joint instability and repeated subluxations.

nodes.description: Formation of thin, widened scars with a papyraceous or cigarette-paper appearance, typically over bony prominences.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and genetic aspects of Ehlers-Danlos syndrome, classic type.
evidence.snippet: Classic Ehlers-Danlos syndrome is a heritable connective tissue disorder characterized by skin hyperextensibility, fragile and soft skin, delayed wound healing with formation of atrophic scars.

evidence.reference_title: New variants in COL5A1 gene among Polish patients with Ehlers-Danlos syndrome: analysis of nine cases.
evidence.snippet: Among all tested patients, nine mutations of COL5A1 gene were detected (8 missense mutations and 1 splice site).

evidence.reference_title: Glycine substitution mutation of COL5A1 in classic Ehlers-Danlos syndrome: a case report and literature review.
evidence.snippet: Glycine substitution mutation of COL5A1 in classic Ehlers-Danlos syndrome: a case report and literature review.

evidence.reference_title: Association of COL5A1 gene polymorphisms and musculoskeletal soft tissue injuries: a meta-analysis based on 21 observational studies.
evidence.snippet: This study supports that rs12722 is associated with an elevated susceptibility to ligament injury, especially in the Caucasian population.

evidence.reference_title: Inherited disorders of collagen gene structure and expression.
evidence.snippet: Mutations in the majority of the 20 known collagen genes have not yet been identified.

evidence.reference_title: COL5A1 / Ehlers-Danlos syndrome, classic type (Definitive)
evidence.snippet: COL5A1 | HGNC:2209 | Ehlers-Danlos syndrome, classic type | MONDO:0007522 | AD | Definitive

nodes.description: Persistent pain in multiple joints resulting from joint instability, subluxations, and degenerative changes.

evidence.reference_title: Orthopaedic manifestations of Ehlers-Danlos syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Thirty patients with Type III Ehlers-Danlos syndrome reported joint pain more frequently than did patients with Types I, II, or IV.

nodes.description: Mutations in the COL5A1 gene lead to defective collagen type V synthesis and assembly.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the COL5A1 gene are causal in the Ehlers-Danlos syndromes I and II.
evidence.snippet: The mutation causes the substitution of the most 5' cysteine residue by a serine within a highly conserved sequence of the pro(alpha)1(V) C-propeptide domain and causes reduction of collagen V by preventing incorporation of the mutant pro(alpha)1(V) chains in the collagen V trimers.

nodes.description: Abnormal collagen type V leads to weakened connective tissues throughout the body.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and genetic aspects of Ehlers-Danlos syndrome, classic type.
evidence.snippet: In the majority of patients with molecularly characterized classic Ehlers-Danlos syndrome, the disease is caused by a mutation leading to a nonfunctional COL5A1 allele and resulting in haploinsufficiency of type V collagen.

evidence.reference_title: Reduced type I collagen utilization: a pathogenic mechanism in COL5A1 haplo-insufficient Ehlers-Danlos syndrome.
evidence.snippet: EDS cells with COL5A1 haplo-insufficiency deposited less than one-half of hydroxyproline as collagen compared to control fibroblasts. ... a reduction of type V collagen, in the EDS derived cells, was associated with the assembly of significantly fewer fibrils compared to control and OI cells.

evidence.reference_title: [The Ehlers-Danlos syndrome: the extracellular matrix scaffold in question].
evidence.snippet: The classical form of the syndrome, which will be principally discussed in this review, can be due to mutations on collagen V, a fibrillar collagen present in small amounts in affected tissues.

nodes.description: Prolonged time for wounds to heal with impaired re-epithelialization and abnormal granulation tissue formation.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and genetic aspects of Ehlers-Danlos syndrome, classic type.
evidence.snippet: Classic Ehlers-Danlos syndrome is a heritable connective tissue disorder characterized by skin hyperextensibility, fragile and soft skin, delayed wound healing with formation of atrophic scars.

nodes.description: Increased susceptibility to bruising from minor trauma due to fragile blood vessels and connective tissue.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and genetic aspects of Ehlers-Danlos syndrome, classic type.
evidence.snippet: Classic Ehlers-Danlos syndrome is a heritable connective tissue disorder characterized by ... easy bruising.

evidence.reference_title: Dermatologic manifestations and diagnostic assessments of the Ehlers-Danlos syndromes: A clinical review.
evidence.snippet: The Ehlers-Danlos syndromes (EDSs) comprise a group of connective tissue disorders that manifest with... easy bruising...

evidence.reference_title: Bleeding and bruising in patients with Ehlers-Danlos syndrome and other collagen vascular disorders.
evidence.snippet: Easy bruising is, to a variable degree, present in all subtypes of EDS...

nodes.description: Insertions or deletions causing a shift in the reading frame and often resulting in a premature stop codon.
Backflow of stomach contents into the esophagus, possibly related to laxity of the lower esophageal sphincter.

evidence.reference_title: Ehlers Danlos syndrome and gastrointestinal manifestations: a 20-year experience at Mayo Clinic.
evidence.snippet: Commonest GI symptoms were: abdominal pain (56.1%), nausea (42.3%), constipation (38.6%), heartburn (37.6%), and irritable bowel syndrome-like symptoms (27.5%)... Among 37.8% of the 378 patients who underwent esophagogastroduodenoscopy, the commonest abnormalities were gastritis, hiatal hernia and reflux esophagitis.

nodes.description: Abnormally increased range of motion in multiple joints due to lax connective tissue.

evidence.reference_title: Ehlers-Danlos Syndromes, Joint Hypermobility and Hypermobility Spectrum Disorders.
evidence.snippet: Currently, musculoskeletal manifestations related to joint hypermobility are perceived as the most prevalent determinants of the quality of life of affected individuals.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular mechanisms of classical Ehlers-Danlos syndrome (EDS).
evidence.snippet: Classical Ehlers-Danlos syndrome (EDS) is a heritable disorder characterized by joint hypermobility, skin hyperextensibility, and abnormal wound healing.

evidence.reference_title: Phenotype of COL3A1/COL5A2 deletion patients.
evidence.snippet: Classical (cEDS) and vascular type (vEDS) are the most prevalent subtypes and are caused by heterozygous pathogenic variants in COL5A1, COL5A2, COL1A1 or, respectively, in COL3A1.

evidence.reference_title: Orthopaedic manifestations of Ehlers-Danlos syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Thirty patients with Type III Ehlers-Danlos syndrome reported joint pain more frequently than did patients with Types I, II, or IV.

nodes.description: Single nucleotide changes resulting in altered amino acid sequence.
Abnormal bulging of the mitral valve leaflets into the left atrium during systole due to connective tissue laxity.

evidence.reference_title: Cross-sectional and longitudinal assessment of aortic root dilation and valvular anomalies in hypermobile and classic Ehlers-Danlos syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Fifteen of the 252 patients (6.0%) had mitral valve prolapse (MVP), although only one patient (0.4%) had MVP that was mild to moderate.

evidence.reference_title: The Heart Muscle and Valve Involvement in Marfan Syndrome, Loeys-Dietz Syndromes, and Collagenopathies.
evidence.snippet: The EDS are currently classified into thirteen subtypes. There is substantial symptoms overlap between the EDS subtypes, and they are associated with an increased incidence of cardiovascular abnormalities, such as mitral valve prolapse and aortic dissection.

nodes.description: Fleshy, scar-associated lesions that develop over pressure points and areas of repeated trauma.

evidence.reference_title: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome related to FKBP14 mutations: detailed cutaneous phenotype.
evidence.snippet: Molluscoid pseudotumours are a characteristic finding in patients with the classic ED variant, but are rarely reported in other variants.

nodes.description: Nearsightedness, a refractive error where distant objects appear blurred.

evidence.reference_title: Ehlers-Danlos syndromes and their manifestations in the visual system.
evidence.snippet: In addition to these commonly recognized phenotypes, recent studies have notably highlighted variable ophthalmic features in EDS.

evidence.reference_title: A frameshift variant in the COL5A1 gene in a cat with Ehlers-Danlos syndrome.
evidence.snippet: The affected cat showed multiple recurrent skin tears, hyperextensibility of the skin and joint abnormalities.

nodes.description: Repeated episodes of joints becoming displaced from their normal positions.

evidence.reference_title: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome and neurological features: a review.
evidence.snippet: Ehlers-Danlos Syndrome is a term that comprises a variety of inherited connective tissue disorders characterized primarily by skin hyperextensibility, joints hypermobility and excessive dislocations, easy bruisability, generalized fragility.

nodes.description: Skin that can be stretched beyond the normal range and returns to its original position when released.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and genetic aspects of Ehlers-Danlos syndrome, classic type.
evidence.snippet: Classic Ehlers-Danlos syndrome is a heritable connective tissue disorder characterized by skin hyperextensibility, fragile and soft skin...

evidence.reference_title: Dermatologic manifestations and diagnostic assessments of the Ehlers-Danlos syndromes: A clinical review.
evidence.snippet: A high prevalence of skin hyperextensibility, bruising, and soft skin were noted.

evidence.reference_title: Precision medicine using whole genome sequencing in a cat identifies a novel COL5A1 variant for classical Ehlers-Danlos syndrome.
evidence.snippet: The classic tragic facial expression was observed as well as chronic pruritus and mild hyperesthesia.

nodes.description: Changes affecting mRNA splicing and leading to exon skipping or intronic retention.

Fragile tissues are prone to damage, leading to manifestations like joint dislocations, skin hyperextensibility, and poor wound healing. Impaired wound healing involves altered mechanosensitive integrin signaling and defective re-epithelialization.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular mechanisms of classical Ehlers-Danlos syndrome (EDS).
evidence.snippet: Classical Ehlers-Danlos syndrome (EDS) is a heritable disorder characterized by joint hypermobility, skin hyperextensibility, and abnormal wound healing.

evidence.reference_title: Bleeding in the heritable connective tissue disorders: mechanisms, diagnosis and treatment.
evidence.snippet: The bleeding tendency is most prominent in the Ehlers-Danlos syndrome (EDS), a heterogeneous group of HDCT sharing clinical manifestations of fragility in skin, ligaments, blood vessels and internal organs.

evidence.reference_title: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome and neurological features: a review.
evidence.snippet: Ehlers-Danlos Syndrome is a term that comprises a variety of inherited connective tissue disorders characterized primarily by skin hyperextensibility, joints hypermobility and excessive dislocations, easy bruisability, generalized fragility.

evidence.reference_title: A "hyperextensive" review of Ehlers-Danlos syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome (EDS) is a heterogeneous group of connective tissue disorders characterized by hyperextensibility, delayed wound healing, joint hypermobility, thin skin, easy bruising, tissue fragility, 'cigarette-paper' scarring over bony prominences.
